---
title: "Syllabus — <Course Title>"
subtitle: "<Term>"
format:
  html: default
  pdf: default
listing:
  - id: timetable-listing
    contents: lectures/*/slides.qmd
    type: table
    fields: [week, date, title, topics]
    field-display-names:
      week: "Week"
      date: "Date"
      title: "Lecture"
      topics: "Topics"
    sort: "date asc"
    filter-ui: false
    page-size: 30
---

## Course information

| | |
|---|---|
| **Title** | <Course Title> |
| **Term** | <Term> |
| **Level** | <Bachelor / Master / PhD> |
| **ECTS** | <NN> |
| **Instructor** | Prof. Dr. Andre Guettler |
| **Contact** | mawi.stuf@uni-ulm.de |
| **Location** | <Room or "online via Zoom"> |
| **Time** | <Day, HH:MM> |
| **Language** | English |

## Course objectives

<2–4 sentences on what the course is about and why it matters.>

## Learning outcomes

By the end of this course, students will be able to:

1. <Outcome 1>
2. <Outcome 2>
3. <Outcome 3>
4. <Outcome 4>

## Prerequisites

- <Prerequisite course or skill>
- <Prerequisite course or skill>

## Required materials

- <Textbook>
- <Reading list>
- <Datasets / software>

## Timetable

::: {#timetable-listing}
:::

> Adding a new lecture folder under `lectures/` automatically updates this table — no manual edits needed.

## Assessment & grading

| Component | Weight |
|---|---|
| Assignments (NN graded sets) | NN% |
| Midterm exam | NN% |
| Final exam | NN% |
| Class participation | NN% |

**Grading scale:** German scale 1.0 (excellent) – 5.0 (fail). 4.0 is passing.

## Policies

### Attendance

<State expectation and any exception policy.>

### Late submissions

<State penalty / grace policy.>

### Academic integrity

All work must be the student's own (or the declared group's). Plagiarism and unauthorized AI use will be referred to the examination office. Citing AI-assisted work where permitted is required.

### Accommodations

Students requiring accommodations should contact the instructor in the first week of term.

## Contact

- **Office hours:** <Day HH:MM, location>.
- **Email:** mawi.stuf@uni-ulm.de — please include the course code in the subject line.
- **Moodle:** all announcements, file drops, and grade postings happen on the course Moodle page.
